name: Frankie Boyle
blurb: He gained national prominence as a regular panellist on the BBC show Mock the Week from 2005 to 2009 before pursuing live tours and his own Channel 4 and BBC programmes.
bio: Frankie Boyle is a Scottish comedian and writer from Glasgow known for caustic, controversial and politically pointed humour. He gained national prominence as a regular panellist on the BBC show Mock the Week from 2005 to 2009 before pursuing live tours and his own Channel 4 and BBC programmes. His comedy frequently provokes debate for its dark and confrontational tone.

timeline.ev: Wins an open-mic event at the Edinburgh Fringe
Becomes a regular on the BBC panel show Mock the Week
Leaves Mock the Week to focus on live work
Launches Frankie Boyles New World Order on BBC Two

accolades: Described by host Dara O Briain as the dark heart of Mock the Week
Sold-out national tours including I Would Happily Punch Every One of You in the Face
Created and hosts Frankie Boyles New World Order
